      <title>Lakers Waive Kobe Bufkin</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285032/Lakers-Waive-Kobe-Bufkin</link>
      <description><p>The Los Angeles Lakers have waived guard Kobe Bufkin from his two-year deal. The Lakers are opening a roster spot ahead of the playoffs to evaluate another signing. </p>
<p>Bufkin appeared in 16 games with the Lakers, averaging 2.9 points in 7.5 minutes.</p>
<p>Bufkin was the 15th overall pick in the 2023 draft out of Michigan.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 13:28:53 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285032/Lakers-Waive-Kobe-Bufkin</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_losangeleslakers.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tyreke Key Waived By Raptors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285033/Tyreke-Key-Waived-By-Raptors</link>
      <description><p>The Toronto Raptors have waived guard Tyreke Key.</p>
<p>Key averaged 14.9 points, 2.5 rebounds, 1.7 assists and 24.5 minutes in 47 games (16 starts) with Raptors 905 in the NBA G League this season.</p>
<p>Key helped the 905 earn the number one seed in the league's Tip-Off Tournament, reach the 2025 G League Winter Showcase Final and secure a playoff berth. Key signed with the Raptors on Apr. 6.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 13:30:25 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285033/Tyreke-Key-Waived-By-Raptors</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_torontoraptors.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Heat Waiving Terry Rozier</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285030/Heat-Waiving-Terry-Rozier</link>
      <description><p>The Miami Heat are waiving guard Terry Rozier on Friday. Rozier has been out all season while be investigated for his involvement in a gambling scandal.</p>
<p>Rozier's contract is fully guaranteed for $26.6 million. That amount will go on Miami's books as dead money. After having his salary initially held in escrow, Rozier has been paid on his regular pay schedule by the Heat.</p>
<p>By waiving Rozier, Miami will open a roster spot ahead of the 2026 postseason. The Heat are locked into a spot in the Eastern Conference Play-In Tournament.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 09:47:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285030/Heat-Waiving-Terry-Rozier</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Rozier_Terry_mia_240226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LeBron James Dealing With Right Hand Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285028/LeBron-James-Dealing-With-Right-Hand-Injury</link>
      <description><p>The Los Angeles Lakers injury-ravaged close to the season continued on Thursday. LeBron James aggravated a right hand injury in the win over the Golden State Warriors. Postgame, James said he's been dealing with the injury for a while now and feels it when he hits the hand in certain ways.</p>
<p>James appeared to re-ding the injury when making a chase-down block midway through the third quarter. Lakers guard Luke Kennard also injured his hand on the play when swiping for the ball. Both James and Kennard returned to the game before exiting midway through the fourth quarter with Los Angeles holding a big lead.</p>
<p>James downplayed the injury postgame by saying, "It'll be fine. I mean it's not fine, but I'm good."</p>
<p> </p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 09:16:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285028/LeBron-James-Dealing-With-Right-Hand-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_260212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>76ers Convert Daley Terry To Standard NBA Contract, Waive Cameron Payne</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285036/76ers-Convert-Daley-Terry-To-Standard-NBA-Contract-Waive-Cameron-Payne</link>
      <description><p>The Philadelphia 76ers have converted guard Daley Terry from a Two-Way contract to a standard NBA deal.</p>
<p>Terry signed his original Two-Way agreement on February 10. In 13 appearances for Philadelphia, he averaged 4.3 points, 1.8 assists, and 1.5 rebounds across 13.0 minutes per game.</p>
<p>In three games with the Delaware Blue Coats, Terry posted 20.0 points, 4.3 rebounds, and 4.0 assists per contest.</p>
<p>The Chicago Bulls selected Terry with the 18th overall in the 2022 NBA Draft. Over four seasons and 204 games in Chicago, he averaged 3.5 points, 1.7 rebounds, and 1.2 assists. He reached double figures in scoring 18 times, including a career-best 17 points on 6-of-8 shooting against Philadelphia on February 24, 2025.</p>
<p>In a corresponding roster move, the 76ers placed guard Cameron Payne on waivers. Payne appeared in 22 games this season, contributing 7.4 points, 2.6 assists, 2.0 rebounds, and 1.1 steals in 17.0 minutes per game.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 22:00:12 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285036/76ers-Convert-Daley-Terry-To-Standard-NBA-Contract-Waive-Cameron-Payne</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_philadelphiasixers.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Shaedon Sharpe, Vit Krejci Eyeing Returns For End of Regular Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285029/Shaedon-Sharpe-Vit-Krejci-Eyeing-Returns-For-End-of-Regular-Season</link>
      <description><p>The Portland Trail Blazers may be getting some reinforcements back at the end of the regular season and ahead of the Western Conference Play-In Tournament. Shaedon Sharpe and Vit Krejci were both upgraded to questionable for Friday's game against the LA Clippers. That contest will go a long way towards deciding which team is #8 and #9 seeds in the Play-In Tournament.</p>
<p>Sharpe has been out since February 6 with a stress reaction in his right fibula. Krejci hasn't played since March 15 due to a left calf contusion.</p>
<p>If Sharpe and Krejci are able to return, the Blazers will be about as healthy as they have been all season long. Only Jerami Grant is sideline due to a calf strain, of players to play for Portland this season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 09:23:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285029/Shaedon-Sharpe-Vit-Krejci-Eyeing-Returns-For-End-of-Regular-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Sharpe_Shaedon_por_221114.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Azzi Fudd Slides To No. 4 In Final WNBA Mock Draft; Awa Fam Holds Top Spot</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285027/Azzi-Fudd-Slides-To-No-4-In-Final-WNBA-Mock-Draft;-Awa-Fam-Holds-Top-Spot</link>
      <description><p>Azzi Fudd falls two spots to fourth overall in the final 2026 WNBA Draft mock ahead of Monday's draft, while Valencia center Awa Fam remains entrenched at No. 1, according to The Athletic. </p>
<p>Fudd's slide follows a challenging NCAA Tournament in which she struggled to generate offense when her jump shot wasn't working, particularly against South Carolina. The Washington Mystics still select her fourth overall, citing her elite shooting ability as she connected on 45 percent of her three-pointers this season, and her growth as a defensive player.</p>
<p>Dallas retains the top pick and is projected to select Fam, who at roughly three years younger than UCLA's Lauren Betts, carries greater long-term upside despite Betts' superior current skill level. Fam's athleticism, shooting touch, and ability to read the floor make her a natural long-term partner alongside Paige Bueckers.</p>
<p>Betts, the Final Four's Most Outstanding Player, slides to Minnesota at No. 2. She averaged 21 points, 9.3 rebounds, and 2.8 blocks while shooting 68.8 percent from the field across six tournament games.</p>
<p>TCU's Olivia Miles holds at No. 3 to Seattle.</p>
<p>"There's like half a dozen people in the world that have her vision that can process it that quickly, that can throw a one-handed bounce pass to the opposite corner, off the dribble, on a laser and then do it with their left hand," said TCU head coach Mark Campbell. </p>
<p>UCLA wing Gabriela Jaquez rises three spots to No. 7 to Portland after posting 21 points, 10 rebounds, and five assists in the national title game. South Carolina's Ta'Niya Latson climbs to No. 8 after a strong tournament showing.</p>
<p>Toronto, picking sixth after winning the offseason coin flip, selects Spanish guard Iyana Martin.</p>
<h2>The Athletic's Final 2026 WNBA Mock Draft</h2>
<p>1. Dallas Wings: Awa Fam, C, Valencia (Spain)<br />2. Minnesota Lynx (from Chicago Sky): Lauren Betts, C, UCLA<br />3. Seattle Storm (from LA Sparks): Olivia Miles, G, TCU<br />4. Washington Mystics: Azzi Fudd, G, UConn<br />5. Chicago Sky (from Connecticut Sun): Kiki Rice, G, UCLA<br />6. Toronto Tempo: Iyana Martin, G, Avenida (Spain)<br />7. Portland Fire: Gabriela Jaquez, F, UCLA<br />8. Golden State Valkyries: Ta'Niya Latson, G, South Carolina<br />9. Washington Mystics (from Seattle Storm): Flau'jae Johnson, G, LSU<br />10. Indiana Fever: Raven Johnson, G, South Carolina<br />11. Washington Mystics (from NY Liberty via Connecticut Sun): Frieda Buhner, G, Movistar Estudiantes (Spain)<br />12. Connecticut Sun (from Phoenix Mercury): Nell Angloma, G, Lattes Montpellier (France)<br />13. Atlanta Dream: Gianna Kneepkens, G, UCLA<br />14. Seattle Storm (from Las Vegas Aces): Cotie McMahon, G/F, Ole Miss<br />15. Connecticut Sun (from Minnesota Lynx via Washington Mystics): Madina Okot, C, South Carolina</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 07:11:00 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285027/Azzi-Fudd-Slides-To-No-4-In-Final-WNBA-Mock-Draft;-Awa-Fam-Holds-Top-Spot</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Fudd_Azzi_uconn_260404.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Lucas Williamson, Grizzlies Sign 2nd 10-Day Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285034/Lucas-Williamson-Grizzlies-Sign-2nd-10-Day-Contract</link>
      <description><p>The Memphis Grizzlies have signed wing Lucas Williamson to a second 10-Day contract.</p>
<p>Williamson (6-4, 205) has appeared in five games (one start) and has averaged 9.0 points, 3.8 rebounds and 2.4 assists in 28.2 minutes since signing his first 10-Day contract on March 31. The 27-year-old has averaged 16.5 points in his last two games.</p>
<p>Williamson competed in 46 games (15 starts) for the Memphis Hustle and Windy City Bulls during the 2025-26 NBA G League Tip-Off Tournament and regular season and averaged 7.2 points in 23.7 minutes.</p>
<p>Williamson went unselected in the 2022 NBA Draft following five years at Loyola Chicago, where he was named the Missouri Valley Conference Defensive Player of the Year twice (2021, 2022) and led the Ramblers to the 2018 NCAA Final Four.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 17:32:57 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285034/Lucas-Williamson-Grizzlies-Sign-2nd-10-Day-Contract</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_memphisgrizzlies.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Billy Donovan On Coaching Future: 'Everyone's Got To Look Out For What Is Best For The Bulls'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285026/Billy-Donovan-On-Coaching-Future-Everyones-Got-To-Look-Out-For-What-Is-Best-For-The-Bulls</link>
      <description><p>Chicago Bulls head coach Billy Donovan responded to team president and CEO Michael Reinsdorf's endorsement that he wants Donovan to remain with the team for next season.</p>
<p>"I think Michael was making the point of how he felt about me," Donovan said on Thursday. "But I also understand that, like I said, everybody's got to look out for what is best for the Bulls at that point in time."</p>
<p>The <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284943/Bulls-Fire-Arturas-Karnisovas-Marc-Eversley-From-Front-Office-Roles">Bulls fired</a> VP of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas and GM Marc Eversley earlier in the week.</p>
<p>Reinsdorf made it known that a future general manager must commit to keeping Donovan as head coach.</p>
<p>"If I interview someone and they're not sold on Billy, they're not sold on a Hall of Fame coach," <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284986/Bulls-Prioritize-Retaining-Billy-Donovan-As-Part-Of-Next-GM-Search">Reinsdorf said</a>. "If Billy wants to be our coach and someone's not interested in that, then they're probably not the right candidate for us."</p>
<p>The 60-year-old has yet to decide on his future and plans to meet with Reinsdorf after the season.</p>
<p>"I love it here," Donovan said. "I've loved the relationship piece. Not only Jerry and Michael, but the people in the building, the staff. It's been an awesome working environment, and I enjoy it. But we're at this place because we really haven't won a lot."</p>
<p>Donovan is set to finish his sixth season with the Bulls.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 01:43:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285026/Billy-Donovan-On-Coaching-Future-Everyones-Got-To-Look-Out-For-What-Is-Best-For-The-Bulls</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Donovan_Billy_chi_240410.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Nneka Ogwumike Chooses Sparks Over Lynx In Free Agency</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285040/Nneka-Ogwumike-Chooses-Sparks-Over-Lynx-In-Free-Agency</link>
      <description><p>Nneka Ogwumike has agreed to rejoin the Los Angeles Sparks after two seasons with the Seattle Storm. The news was first reported by her sister, Chiney Ogwumike, an ESPN analyst.</p>
<p>Ogwumike also visited the Minnesota Lynx before settling on a return to Los Angeles as she enters her 15th professional season. Ogwumike is a 10-time All-Star. </p>
<p>The veteran forward spent her first 12 seasons with the Sparks before departing for Seattle ahead of the 2024 campaign. Last season she averaged 18 points, 7 rebounds, and 2 assists while shooting 51 percent from the floor in 31 minutes per game.</p>
<p>Ogwumike's most decorated season in Los Angeles came in 2016, when the Sparks claimed the WNBA championship and she earned league MVP honors, posting career bests across points, rebounds, assists, and shooting percentage.</p>
<p>Her return gives second-year coach Lynne Roberts a proven veteran anchor. The Sparks have already retained guard Kelsey Plum on a core designation and hold no first-round draft pick, signaling a roster built around experienced players.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 22:39:25 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285040/Nneka-Ogwumike-Chooses-Sparks-Over-Lynx-In-Free-Agency</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/wnba_losangelessparks.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Arike Ogunbowale Takes Below-Supermax Deal To Stay With Wings</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285039/Arike-Ogunbowale-Takes-Below-Supermax-Deal-To-Stay-With-Wings</link>
      <description><p>Arike Ogunbowale is returning to the Dallas Wings on a multiyear, seven-figure contract, sources told ESPN Friday. The four-time WNBA All-Star opted for below the available $1.4 million supermax to allow the franchise flexibility in building a competitive roster around her.</p>
<p>The Wings had extended Ogunbowale a core qualifying offer earlier this week. She was selected by Dallas with the fifth overall pick in 2019 out of Notre Dame and remains committed to the city and franchise.</p>
<p>During her tenure with the Wings, Ogunbowale has earned three All-WNBA selections, two All-Star Game MVP awards, and led the league in scoring in 2020. She averaged at least 18.7 points per game in every season prior to 2025, when she posted a career-low 15.5 points along with 4.1 assists and 2.5 rebounds as Dallas finished last in the standings.</p>
<p>With new head coach Jose Fernandez leading the team, Dallas holds the No. 1 pick in Monday's collegiate draft and figures to pair Ogunbowale with 2025 top selection Paige Bueckers as the core of a playoff push.</p>
<p>Ogunbowale also claimed an Unrivaled title with Mist B.C. during the offseason.</p>
<p>In a separate move, forward Jessica Shepard has agreed to a multiyear deal with Dallas, sources told ESPN. Shepard, Ogunbowale's former Notre Dame teammate and national championship partner, spent five seasons with the Minnesota Lynx after being drafted in 2019. The 6-foot-4 forward averaged 8.0 points, 7.3 rebounds, and 2.6 assists off the bench in 2025.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 22:33:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285039/Arike-Ogunbowale-Takes-Below-Supermax-Deal-To-Stay-With-Wings</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/wnba_dallaswings.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kelsey Mitchell, Fever Sign One-Year, $1.4M Supermax</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285038/Kelsey-Mitchell-Fever-Sign-One-Year-$14M-Supermax</link>
      <description><p>Kelsey Mitchell and the Indiana Fever have agreed to a one-year, $1.4 million supermax agreement.</p>
<p>Mitchell was an All-WNBA selection in 2025, helping the Fever into the WNBA semifinals despite the injury absence of Caitlin Clark and Sophie Cunningham. Mitchell finished fifth in MVP voting.</p>
<p>Mitchell became the first Fever player to average 20 points in the regular season to go with 1.8 rebounds and 3.4 assists. Mitchell had a PER of 19.9. </p>
<p>The Fever selected Mitchell with the second overall pick in the 2018 draft out of Ohio State. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 22:22:47 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285038/Kelsey-Mitchell-Fever-Sign-One-Year-$14M-Supermax</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/wnba_indianafever.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Chelsea Gray, Aces Sign Three-Year, $3M Deal</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285037/Chelsea-Gray-Aces-Sign-Three-Year-$3M-Deal</link>
      <description><p>Chelsea Gray has agreed to a three-year, $3 million fully guaranteed deal to return to the Las Vegas Aces. Gray averaged 11.2 points, 5.4 rebounds and 3.9 assists in 2025 while helping Las Vegas to its third title in four years.</p>
<p>Gray was named MVP of the WNBA Finals in 2022.</p>
<p>Gray is a six-time All-Star and was the 11th overall pick in the 2014 draft out of Duke.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 22:14:54 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285037/Chelsea-Gray-Aces-Sign-Three-Year-$3M-Deal</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/wnba_lasvegasaces.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Alex Sarr Likely Out For Season With Toe Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285025/Alex-Sarr-Likely-Out-For-Season-With-Toe-Injury</link>
      <description><p>Washington Wizards forward Alex Sarr is likely to miss the team's final three games of the season with a toe injury, head coach Brian Keefe said on Thursday.</p>

      <title>Jackie Young Becomes First WNBA Player To Sign Million Dollar Contract In Return To Aces</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285022/Jackie-Young-Becomes-First-WNBA-Player-To-Sign-Million-Dollar-Contract-In-Return-To-Aces</link>
      <description><p>Four-time All-Star guard Jackie Young is finalizing a one-year agreement to return to the Las Vegas Aces worth $1.19 million, sources told ESPN Thursday, making her the first player in WNBA history to sign a million dollar contract.</p>
<p>Young did not take meetings with any other teams despite being one of the most sought-after free agents this offseason. Her return keeps the Aces' core intact alongside four-time MVP A'ja Wilson, who has already stated her intent to remain in Las Vegas, and six-time All-Star Chelsea Gray.</p>
<p>The agreement preserves the franchise that has won three championships in four years, including the 2025 title.</p>
<p>Young, 27, has spent her entire professional career with Las Vegas after being selected first overall in the 2019 draft out of Notre Dame. She earned Most Improved Player honors in 2022 and has been named to four All-Star teams and two All-WNBA squads. In 2025 she averaged 16.5 points, 5.1 assists, 4.5 rebounds and 1.3 steals per game.</p>
<p>The 6-foot guard is also a two-time Olympic gold medalist, winning with the U.S. 3-on-3 team in Tokyo and the 5-on-5 team in Paris. She won a national championship at Notre Dame in 2018.</p>
<p>Young's contract marks a landmark moment for the WNBA as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284484/New-WNBA-CBA-Features-$600K-Average-Salary-$7M-Salary-Cap-20-perc-Revenue-Sharing">the league enters a new era following its most recent collective bargaining agreement</a>, one designed to substantially increase player compensation at the top of the market.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Stanford's Ebuka Okorie Declares For NBA Draft After Record Freshman Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285021/Stanfords-Ebuka-Okorie-Declares-For-NBA-Draft-After-Record-Freshman-Season</link>
      <description><p>Stanford guard Ebuka Okorie announced Thursday he will enter the NBA Draft following a record-breaking freshman season in which he led the ACC in scoring and established himself as one of the top prospects in the country.</p>
<p>Okorie, who arrived at Stanford with no high-major scholarship offers out of New Hampshire, averaged 23.2 points per game to finish eighth nationally in scoring. The only freshmen ahead of him were projected lottery picks AJ Dybantsa of BYU and Darius Acuff Jr. of Arkansas.</p>
<p>"A year ago you guys took a chance on a kid from New Hampshire with zero high major offers, welcoming me with open arms and allowing me to be the best version of myself on and off the court," Okorie said in a social media post thanking coach Kyle Smith and the Stanford staff.</p>
<p>Okorie made an immediate impact, scoring 26 points in his debut against Portland State before breaking out with a 36-point effort in an upset win over North Carolina in January. He scored 40 points in a home victory over Georgia Tech and finished with eight 30-point games on the season, breaking the ACC freshman record previously held by Duke's Marvin Bagley III.</p>
<p>His 719 total points rank third in Stanford history for a single season, trailing only Adam Keefe's 734 in 1991-92 and Chasson Randle's 724 in 2014-15.</p>
<p>Okorie earned first-team All-ACC honors and honorable mention AP All-American recognition, adding 3.6 rebounds, 3.6 assists and 1.6 steals per game. The last player to reach those combined per-game marks was Ja Morant at Murray State in 2018-19.</p>
<p>His perimeter shooting also improved significantly as the season progressed, connecting on 46.9% of his three-point attempts over his final 12 games. He is projected as a late first-round selection.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:45:47 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Stanford.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Clears Kings, Says Late-Game Foul On Seth Curry Was Coaching Error, Not Tanking</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285020/NBA-Clears-Kings-Says-Late-Game-Foul-On-Seth-Curry-Was-Coaching-Error-Not-Tanking</link>
      <description><p>The NBA has cleared Sacramento Kings coach Doug Christie of tanking following an investigation into his decision to foul Golden State Warriors guard Seth Curry in the final minutes of Tuesday's 110-105 loss, determining the move was a tactical miscalculation rather than a deliberate effort to lose.</p>
<p>"The investigation found that Christie made no intentional effort to give the Warriors a shooting foul, or to cause the Kings to lose the game," the league said in a statement.</p>
<p>The foul, ordered with 3:15 remaining and the Kings trailing by one, sent Curry to the free-throw line and set off a social media firestorm accusing Sacramento of blatant tanking. Draymond Green amplified the controversy after the game, citing the decision as further evidence of the league's broader competitive integrity problem.</p>
<p>Christie and Kings officials maintained from the outset that the foul was a straightforward coaching mistake. Christie intended to preserve a timeout before automatically losing it when the clock dropped below three minutes, planning to draw up a play on the subsequent possession. He was unaware the Kings were already in the bonus, which sent Curry to the line rather than granting a side-out inbounds.</p>
<p>The strategy behind the foul ultimately worked. Christie drew up a play for Doug McDermott out of the timeout that resulted in a corner three-pointer, putting Sacramento ahead by three with 2:53 remaining. The Kings ultimately surrendered that lead in the closing minutes.</p>
<p>The broader evidence also supports Christie's account. Since sitting at 12-46 on Feb. 21, Sacramento has gone 9-13, a stretch inconsistent with a team actively pursuing losses. Christie has been direct on the subject.</p>
<p>"Tanking is the last thing I'd do," Christie said after a March 15 win over Utah. "I respect the game too much. These young men, when you do things like that, it hurts them."</p>
<p>"If we wanted to tank, we're doing an awful job of it," said one source with the Kings.</p>
<p>The episode arrives as the NBA continues to struggle with the broader tanking dilemma. The Utah Jazz and Indiana Pacers were fined $500,000 and $100,000 respectively in February for violating the league's player participation policy. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors">Commissioner Adam Silver has signaled significant lottery reform is coming for next season</a>, though those measures have yet to take effect.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:40:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285020/NBA-Clears-Kings-Says-Late-Game-Foul-On-Seth-Curry-Was-Coaching-Error-Not-Tanking</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Christie_Doug_sac_241227.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Jaylen Brown Turned Celtics' Gap-Year Season Into 'Opportunity To Show World What I Could Do'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285014/Jaylen-Brown-Turned-Celtics-Gap-Year-Season-Into-Opportunity-To-Show-World-What-I-Could-Do</link>
      <description><p>When the Boston Celtics lost three starters from their championship roster and Jayson Tatum ruptured his Achilles tendon, the league largely wrote off the 2025-26 season as a transitional year, but Jaylen Brown had a different interpretation entirely.</p>
<p>"From a financial standpoint, this was a rebuild, right?" Brown told ESPN's Ramona Shelburne. "But I didn't look at it like that. I looked at it as an opportunity to show the world who I am and what I could do."</p>
<p>Brown has done exactly that as is posting career highs in points, rebounds and assists, carries the second-highest usage rate in the NBA and has guided Boston to the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference, a level of success almost no one predicted when the season began.</p>
<p>The foundation for what Brown has built this season was laid in isolation last summer. Three weeks after undergoing surgery to repair a torn meniscus, he was alone in his Boston home, turning away calls from friends, teammates and family. He did not want comfort. He wanted space.</p>
<p>"One of my toxic traits is that I have a hard time letting people see me weak," Brown said.</p>
<p>During that rehab, Brown meditated and studied his teammates' astrological charts and numerology in an effort to customize his approach to each personality in the locker room. Brown also did red light therapy on his knee several times daily.</p>
<p>Brad Stevens reminded Brown that the franchise had <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Boston-Celtics/2/roster-turnover">rebuilt its roster several times</a> since drafting him third overall in 2017, and that Boston still expected to compete despite everything that had changed.</p>
<p>"A lot of guys would have misconstrued that and not done what he's done," Stevens told ESPN. "And what he's done is he's played great, and he's empowered others. We needed him to do both for our team to be really good."</p>
<p>Stevens told Brown that the key was recognizing what the new pieces around him were capable of.</p>
<p>"The only thing that a lot of these guys were, was unproven," Stevens said. "I think Brown knew Jordan Walsh could play. That Baylor Scheierman could play. That Neemias Queta and Luka Garza could play. But he also knew that by showing belief in them, he would get a lot out of them."</p>
<p>Brown took that charge seriously. He hosted team dinners. He mentored younger players individually. He advocated publicly for Queta's Most Improved Player and All-Defensive Team candidacy. Walsh, who calls Brown "Unc" because of the mentorship they've built, became one of several teammates to elevate alongside him.</p>
<p>Boston's numbers reflect the collaborative nature of Brown's influence. The Celtics post an effective field goal percentage of 65.2% off passes from Brown, fifth highest among players with 500 or more assists this season. Teammates Payton Pritchard, Sam Hauser, Derrick White and Queta are all averaging career highs in scoring.</p>
<p>"Jaylen has believed in this group from the get-go, and that includes the summer and that includes when we were 0-3," Stevens said. "I think that's helped bring out the best in all of those guys. He has seemed to find a lot of joy in helping them prove themselves."</p>
<p>For much of his career, Brown's edge came from perceived slights, trade rumors and a sense that the broader basketball world undervalued him. This season, something has shifted. </p>
<p>"At times, I think I would make myself small, for other people to feel comfortable," Brown said. "There's a difference between that and making yourself small and dimming your light."</p>
<p>Tatum returned from his Achilles injury in March and has watched up close what Brown built in his absence. He has no doubt about what drove it.</p>
<p>"Obviously he's somebody that's always been capable," Tatum told ESPN. "This was just an opportunity where more was required from everybody, but especially him. The NBA is all about opportunity and the guys who really make the most out of it. The special ones do, and that's exactly what he's been able to do this year."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 12:48:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285014/Jaylen-Brown-Turned-Celtics-Gap-Year-Season-Into-Opportunity-To-Show-World-What-I-Could-Do</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Jaylen_bos_250504.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>AJ Dybantsa Moves To No. 1 In ESPN's 2026 NBA Draft Big Board, Top Of Class Remains Open</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285013/AJ-Dybantsa-Moves-To-No-1-In-ESPNs-2026-NBA-Draft-Big-Board-Top-Of-Class-Remains-Open</link>
      <description><p>AJ Dybantsa has claimed the top position in ESPN's updated 2026 NBA Draft rankings, displacing Darryn Peterson atop the big board following a strong close to his freshman season at BYU that solidified his standing among league executives.</p>
<p>Dybantsa, a 6-foot-9 wing, averaged 25.5 points, 6.8 rebounds and 3.7 assists across 35 games, demonstrating an ability to carry a significant offensive workload while showing growth as a decision-maker. </p>
<p>"Dybantsa has become the player NBA executives most commonly project to be selected first, with the expectation that he'll be a star wing scorer with the capacity to drive winning on both sides of the ball in the long run," <a href="https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/46886245/2026-nba-draft-big-board-rankings-top-100-prospects-players">wrote Jeremy Woo</a>. "In a draft replete with high-level talent, he has positioned himself as the easiest answer at No. 1."</p>
<p>Despite ceding ground in the No. 1 conversation, Peterson remains a compelling candidate. Scouts view the Kansas freshman as the most gifted scorer and shotmaker in the class, though a full-body cramping episode in the preseason clouded his freshman year both physically and mentally. His medical report and predraft interviews will carry significant weight as teams assess his long-term availability.</p>
<p>Duke's Cameron Boozer holds the third position after <a href="shman-To-Win-AP-Player-Of-The-Year">a dominant season that included Naismith and AP Player of the Year honors</a>. Boozer led college basketball with a 17.1 box plus-minus score and drew comparisons from scouts to Domantas Sabonis and Kevin Love, though questions persist about his defensive ceiling relative to the other top options.</p>
<p>North Carolina's Caleb Wilson sits fourth despite missing the postseason with hand injuries, while Illinois guard Keaton Wagler cemented his lottery standing with a Final Four run. Arkansas point guard Darius Acuff Jr. vaulted to sixth after winning SEC Player of the Year and leading the Razorbacks to a Sweet 16 berth.</p>
<p>One significant storyline heading into the predraft process is roster attrition. NIL earnings and transfer portal flexibility continue to make returning to college an appealing financial decision for many underclassmen, creating uncertainty around the depth of the available pool beyond the lottery. The NCAA's early entry withdrawal deadline is May 27.</p>
<p>The draft combine and lottery are scheduled for May 10, with the draft itself expected in late June. The past two <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_winners">NBA draft lottery winners</a> had a three percent chance or worse to win the lottery, while the previous four seasons were won by teams that finished in the bottom-3 with 14 percent odds.</p>
